name: Average Electric Vehicle Purchase Price
description: Average transaction price for a new battery electric vehicle (BEV) in the United States. Tracks overall EV affordability driven by battery cost reductions, manufacturing scale, and increasing model diversity.

data_points.source: Kelley Blue Book / DOE — Nissan Leaf, Tesla Model S early sales; weighted average ~$38K
KBB/Cox Automotive — limited BEV selection, mostly Leaf and Model S
KBB/Cox Automotive — Chevrolet Bolt introduction; Tesla Model 3 not yet available
KBB/Cox Automotive — Tesla Model 3 initially at higher trims driving average up
KBB/Cox Automotive — Model 3 Standard Range available, bringing average down slightly
KBB/Cox Automotive — EV average transaction price rising with luxury EV launches
KBB/Cox Automotive — chip shortage and high demand driving prices up across industry
KBB/Cox Automotive — peak EV pricing: average BEV transaction price reached ~$65K (July 2022)
KBB/Cox Automotive — Tesla price cuts, increased competition, BEV average dropping to ~$50K
KBB/Cox Automotive — continued price competition, more affordable models (Equinox EV, Model 3 Highland), average ~$44K (approx)
